name: Butternut Squash Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the butternut squash in half lengthwise and scoop out the seeds.
2. Arrange the halves cut side down in a roasting pan sprayed with nonstick vegetable oil spray.
3. Bake the squash in the oven for 40-45 minutes or until very tender. Set aside to cool.
4. When the squash is completely cool, scoop the flesh from the skin.
5. In a saucepan, cook the onion and ginger (if using) over moderately low heat for 5 minutes or until the onion is softened.
6. Add the chicken broth to the saucepan and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes, covered.
7. Add the squash pulp to the saucepan.
8. Transfer the mixture to a blender or food processor in batches and puree until smooth. Return any unused puree to the saucepan.
9. Add enough water to achieve the desired consistency, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
10. Cook the soup over moderate heat until hot.
11. Garnish each portion with a heaping teaspoon of low-fat sour cream.
